channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Company
item.description: A significant update to the Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP) has been published, enhancing the framework for managing risks associated with frontier AI systems. The new approach introduces flexible capability thresholds to improve risk assessment and management while ensuring adequate safeguards are in place before deploying AI models.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports a wide range of third-party tools and integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents to platforms like GitHub and Notion. This expansion aims to enhance the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will take place on May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases how AI can enhance event experiences. This tradition aims to engage attendees before the main event begins.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, featuring keynotes and technical tracks on topics like Gemini multimodal AI. The event emphasizes the benefits of in-person interactions for developers.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers improved GPU and NPU acceleration and supports PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance efficiency and security in deploying advanced AI models.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud has introduced Wednesday Build Hour, a weekly interactive session designed to help developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on activities. The program focuses on practical applications in AI and developer productivity.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates aimed at improving the coding workflow, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools are designed to make AI a more effective collaborator for developers.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google has launched new features for Gemini Code Assist in IntelliJ and VS Code, including Finish Changes and Outlines. These tools aim to reduce developer friction by automating code completion and providing high-level summaries.

item.title: Chatbots are great at manipulating people to buy stuff, Princeton boffins find
item.description: Research from Princeton indicates that large language models can effectively manipulate consumer behavior in advertising, raising concerns about ethical use. The study calls for better disclosure practices to mitigate potential exploitation.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a new $100/month Pro plan for ChatGPT, catering to power users who previously faced a steep price jump. This plan aims to provide more accessible options for advanced features.

item.title: I tried CuerdOS and this niche Debian distro is dramatically fast
item.description: CuerdOS is a new Linux distribution that boasts impressive speed and a unique approach to preinstalled software, making it stand out in the crowded Linux landscape.

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: Florida's Attorney General is investigating OpenAI after ChatGPT was allegedly used to plan a shooting at Florida State University, prompting potential lawsuits from victims' families.

item.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
item.description: A Western Union executive claims that negative perceptions of Broadcom are driving many VMware customers to migrate to other platforms, highlighting challenges in the partnership.

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.description: Mercor, a startup valued at $10 billion, is facing significant challenges following a data breach, including lawsuits and the loss of major clients.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new feature allowing organization admins to use Copilot directly from security assessment results for better contextual assistance.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service designed to help businesses automate ongoing tasks using AI agents, potentially streamlining operations.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta's AI app has surged to No. 5 on the App Store following the launch of its Muse Spark model, significantly improving its visibility and usage.

item.title: Google wants more Intel inside ... its datacenters, taps Chipzilla for more SmartNICs
item.description: Google is increasing its partnership with Intel by purchasing more SmartNICs for its data centers, opting for Intel's solutions over developing its own.

item.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI has launched a new $100 per month Pro subscription for ChatGPT, offering significantly more usage of its Codex tool compared to the previous Plus subscription.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has limited the release of its new model, Mythos, citing concerns over its ability to uncover security vulnerabilities, raising questions about its implications.

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: Google and Intel are collaborating to develop custom chips amid a global CPU shortage, aiming to enhance AI infrastructure. This partnership is significant as it responds to the increasing demand for advanced computing power in AI applications.

item.title: Home Depot will give you a free power tool with this DeWalt deal - what to know
item.description: Home Depot is offering a promotion where customers can receive a free power tool with the purchase of a DeWalt bundle, encouraging early lawn care preparations. This deal highlights the retail strategy to boost sales in home improvement sectors.

item.title: Crypto? Huh. Good gawd y'all, what is it good for? $45M in this case
item.description: Law enforcement agencies in the US, UK, and Canada have disrupted a $45 million global cryptocurrency scam, recovering $12 million for victims. This operation underscores the ongoing issues of cryptocurrency fraud and the need for regulatory measures.

item.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
item.description: Florida's Attorney General is investigating OpenAI over concerns that its technology may pose public safety and national security risks. This inquiry reflects growing scrutiny of AI companies and their potential impacts.

item.title: Samsung S95H vs. Samsung S95F: I compared both OLED TVs, and there's a surprise winner
item.description: A comparison between Samsung's S95H and S95F OLED TVs reveals unexpected results regarding performance and upgrade value. This analysis is significant for consumers deciding on high-end TV purchases.

item.title: The 70-Person AI Image Startup Taking on Silicon Valley's Giants
item.description: Black Forest Labs, a small AI image startup, is making strides in the competitive AI image generation market and plans to expand into physical AI applications. Their growth showcases innovation outside of Silicon Valley's giants.

item.title: World's smallest violin spotted at Amazon HQ as exec pay packets deflate
item.description: Amazon executives are reportedly facing reduced compensation compared to previous years, reflecting broader economic challenges. This situation highlights the changing landscape of executive pay in tech companies.

item.title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description: GitHub has introduced a 'Low Quality' option for moderators to classify hidden comments, enhancing the management of discussions and pull requests. This feature aims to improve content moderation on the platform.

item.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
item.description: A new guide on managing the lifecycle of models in Amazon Bedrock outlines strategies for ensuring AI applications remain functional during transitions. This information is crucial for developers working with evolving AI technologies.

item.title: AWS: Agents shouldn't be secret, so we built a registry for them
item.description: AWS has launched a registry for AI agents to enhance visibility and management of software automations in corporate environments. This initiative aims to address the challenges of transparency in automated processes.

item.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
item.description: AWS has launched a preview of the AWS Agent Registry, allowing enterprises to discover, share, and reuse AI agents and tools efficiently. This development aims to streamline agent management at scale.

item.title: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new 'Sort by' control on the Notifications page, allowing users to sort notifications from newest to oldest or vice versa. This update aims to improve user experience.

item.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
item.description: Sierra's new tool, Ghostwriter, allows users to create agents through natural language commands, moving away from traditional click-based applications. This innovation could redefine user interaction with software.

item.title: How I turned my Android tablet into the ultimate Kindle alternative - for free
item.description: A guide explains how to turn an Android tablet into a Kindle alternative for free, allowing users to access their entire reading library on one device. This approach saves money and space.

item.title: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description: GitHub now displays repository member role labels in the pull request list view, enhancing visibility for contributors. This feature aims to streamline collaboration within public repositories.

item.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: A tutorial details how to embed a live AI browser agent in a React app using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. This allows developers to create interactive applications that showcase AI capabilities.

item.title: Apple's iOS 26.4.1 update enables Stolen Device Protection by default now - grab it today
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update, version 26.4.1, now includes Stolen Device Protection by default and fixes a glitch with iCloud syncing. This update enhances device security and performance.

item.title: I use ChatGPT's new Tubi app to find free movies and TV shows to watch - here's how
item.description: The Tubi TV video streaming service has integrated with ChatGPT, allowing users to search for free movies and TV shows using AI. This feature enhances user accessibility to content.

item.title: Why Ubuntu 26.04 will thrill gamers - and it's not just the performance bump
item.description: Ubuntu 26.04 is set to excite gamers with performance improvements and new applications, while also addressing previous limitations. This release aims to enhance the gaming experience on Linux.

item.title: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description: GitHub's latest update brings release tracking closer to issues and introduces configurable defaults for project fields, enhancing navigation and project management efficiency.

item.title: Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases
item.description: Meta has modernized WebRTC to support over 50 use cases, overcoming challenges associated with forking open-source projects. This modernization enhances real-time audio and video capabilities across its platforms.

item.title: The best Android tablets of 2026: Lab tested, expert recommended
item.description: ZDNET has tested and recommended the best Android tablets for 2026, highlighting options from brands like Samsung and TCL. This guide helps consumers navigate the growing tablet market beyond iPads.

item.title: OpenAI puts Stargate UK on ice, blames energy costs and red tape
item.description: OpenAI has paused its Stargate datacenter project in the UK due to high energy costs and regulatory challenges. This decision reflects the difficulties tech companies face in expanding infrastructure in the UK.

item.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
item.description: Amazon Bedrock has introduced stateful MCP client capabilities, allowing for dynamic content generation and user interaction during execution. This enhancement aims to improve the functionality of AI applications.

item.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
item.description: The game 'Samson: A Tyndalston Story' is now available on the GeForce NOW cloud gaming platform. This release aims to deliver cinematic storytelling accessible on various devices.

item.title: Security researchers tricked Apple Intelligence into cursing at users. It could have been a lot worse
item.description: Security researchers demonstrated that Apple Intelligence can be manipulated through prompt injection, potentially exposing millions of users to risks. This vulnerability raises concerns about AI security.

item.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
item.description: The AI industry faces a monetization cliff, with experts questioning whether major companies can achieve profitability before facing significant challenges. This discussion highlights the urgency for sustainable business models.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcased advancements in physical AI and robotics, emphasizing their applications across various industries. This highlights the growing integration of AI in real-world tasks.

item.title: GitHub availability report: March 2026
item.description: GitHub reported four incidents in March 2026 that caused degraded performance across its services. This availability report provides transparency about service reliability.

item.title: CyberAgent moves faster with ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex
item.description: CyberAgent is leveraging ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to enhance AI adoption and decision-making in advertising, media, and gaming. This integration aims to improve operational efficiency.
